sutta: a certain mung approached the exalted 1 paid homage etc and sat down and said lord well worried for me if the exalted 1 would teach me dhamma briefly after hearing it i would abide alone secluded jealous earnest resolved and the buddha said but this is just how some foolish fellows beg of me and when they have heard me preach dhamma they think i am just the 1 to be followed [Music] and the monk said lord let the exalted 1 teach me dhamma briefly let the welfare teach me dhamma briefly perhaps i might understand the purpose of the exalted 1's word perhaps i might i might become an heir to the word of the exalted 1 and the buddha said wherefore monk you must train yourself thus inwardly my mind shall become firm and well composed and evil and unwholesome states which arise and overwhelm the mind shall find no footing thus indeed must you train yourself i just stopped here for a moment this advice the buddha gave is a very good advice is telling the manga to make the mind firm and well composed so that the evil and unwholesome states of mind can find no footing to arise that is the purpose of samadhi strong concentration and the purpose is that these hindrances these unwholesome states of mind shall not arise because they have no footing to arise now the buddha continues when monk inwardly your mind is firm and well composed and evil and unwholesome states which arise and overwhelm the mind find no footing then you must train yourself thus radiation of good will by the mind shall become made become by me continuously developed made a vehicle of made a basis exercised augmented thoroughly set going thus indeed must you train yourself when this concentration is thus may become and when and sorry when among this concentration is thus may become and develop by you then you should make become this concentration with initial and sustained application make it become without initial application but with sustained application only make it become without either initial or sustained application make it become with rap rapture or delight pt make it become without rapture make it become accompanied by pleasure sukkah make it become accompanied by equanimity when monk this concentration is made become by you and well may become then monk you must train yourself thus radiation of compassion joy equanimity by the mind shall be made become by me continuously developed made a vehicle of made a basis exercised augmented thoroughly set going thus indeed must you train yourself when among these concentrations are thus may become and develop by you then you should make these concentrations become with initial and sustained application etc make them become accompanied by equanimity [Music] i'll just stop here for a moment so here you see the buddha is advising the manga firstly to make the mind firm and composed then after that he asked the monk to practice radiation of goodwill by the mind meta good will or loving kindness to develop the meta and then after that to develop the concentration without sorry with initial and sustained application that is vitaka and vichara that make it become without initial application but which sustained application that make it become without either initial or sustained application that is the second jhana with initial and sustained application is the first jhana without initial and sustained application is the second genre and make it become with delight or rapture pt and then make it without pt and then make it accompanied by pleasure accompanied by equanimity these are different stages of jhana going from the first to the second to the third to the fourth jhana because the fourth jana is a state of pure equanimity and mindfulness and then after that the buddha advised the monk to practice compassion joy and equanimity the other 4 brahma viharas these are all the brahma viharas and after developing the practicing it to practice the concentrations 1 by 1 now to continue when monk these concentrations are made become by you and may well become then monk you must train yourself thus as to the body looking upon the body i will live strenuous mindful and aware overcoming the hankering and dejection common in this world thus indeed mung you must train yourself when mount this concentration is thus may become and develop by you then make this concentration become with an initial and sustained application without initial application but with sustained application then without initial and sustained application etc when among this concentration is made become by you and well-made become then monk you must train yourself thus as to feelings looking upon feelings etc as to the mind looking upon mine as to dhamma looking upon dhamma i will live strenuous self-possessed and mindful overcoming the hankering and dejection common in this world thus indeed you must train yourself stop here for a moment so here the buddha is advising the manga after developing the concentration to practice the sati patana mindfulness with regard to abide contemplating the body contemplating feeling contemplating mind and contemplating dhamma these are the 4 objects of sati patana then the buddha continues when this concentration is thus may become and developed by you then you should make this concentration become with initial and sustained application make it become without initial application but we sustain application only make you become without either initial or sustained application make it become with rapture make it become without rapture make it become accompanied with pleasure make it become accompanied with equanimity when among this concentration is made become by you and well may become then just to whatever place you go you shall go in comfort wherever you stand you shall stand in comfort wherever you sit you shall sit in comfort and wherever you make your bed you shall lie down in comfort and the mouth roused by the exalted 1's exhortation cut up from his seat saluted the exalted 1 and departed passing him by on his right now not long after dwelling alone secluded serious earnest resolved that monk attained to and abort in that unsurpassed goal of the holy life realizing it by personal knowledge in this life for the sake of which clansmen rightly go forth from the home to the homeless life and he fully realized birth is destroyed lived is the holy life done is what had to be done there is more no more of this to come and that monk was numbered among the arahants as the end of the sutta
commentary: so in this last part the buddha is again asking the monk to practice their concentrations in in in stages and very systematically and then the buddha says when he has developed up to the concentration accompanied by equanimity which is the fourth jana then the buddha says when this concentration is made become or develop by you and well may become then monk just to whatever place you shall go you shall go in comfort wherever you stand you shall stand in comfort wherever you sit you shall sit in comfort and when wherever you make your bed you shall lie down in comfort this is the result of attaining the fort jana it's the buddha has also mentioned this and it shows that the effect of samadhi lasts for a while it's not that immediately you come out of samadhi all the hindrances come back and it is precisely because the hindrances are in obedience that this monk can go everywhere in comfort in comfort means he's not disturbed by the hindrances by the defilements of mind as i mentioned earlier in another suta it was quite plain the buddha said even an external ascetic mention of a external ascetic by the name of sunita the buddha said because he had developed the jhanas he was free of the passions which are the basically the hindrances so that's the end of the suitable you notice that the buddha is a very systematic teacher you see it talks about attaining the jhanas in very different many different levels he's very very systematic you can see and he asked the monk to practice the first stage with initial and sustained application then get rid of the initial application and go into that state with sustained application only and then after that to go to the next stage without initial or sustained application and then after that the next stage with bt and then the next state without pt and then the negative with sukkah pleasure and then after the next state after that after dropping sukkah then you abide in equanimity so you can see the buddha is a very systematic teacher he practiced very systematically and he taught very systematically that is the 1 of the advantages of studying the sutasa we we learn to do things very systematically according to the teachings of the buddha the nexuta
